issues.title: Coverage: Users: loans (list, create, get, renew, change due date)

tests.scope: Exercise the read-only loan methods shipped in issue #40 against live SANDBOX. Calls list_user_loans(${existing_user_primary_id}) to confirm the GET /users/{user_id}/loans endpoint round-trips and returns a List[Dict] envelope (the wrapper returns List[Dict] per the chunk's audit-corrected signature; the SANDBOX user is likely to have zero active loans on a fresh fixture, in which case the list is empty and that is still a pass for the list-shape assertion). If the returned list is non-empty, picks the first loan's loan_id and calls get_user_loan(${existing_user_primary_id}, <loan_id>) to confirm the single-loan GET round-trips and returns a non-empty Dict[str, Any] containing a 'loan_id' key. If the list is empty, the get-step is skipped — the test is still considered pass because the list endpoint itself responded correctly. No state is mutated. Covers the read half of issue #40's integration coverage; the create→get→renew live cycle is exercised by t-40-3 and update_user_loan is documented as unmappable below.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read-only and write smoke testing. Used by t-40-1 (read-only list/get) and t-40-3 (live create→get→renew loan round-trip — leaves a permanent loan artifact, see t-40-3 scope and the operator-cleanup banner). The user is not deleted or otherwise destructively mutated; t-40-3 creates a real loan that the operator must manually return via the Alma staff UI after the run because Alma exposes NO DELETE endpoint for loans. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
users = Users(client)
loans = users.list_user_loans('${existing_user_primary_id}')

first_loan = None
loan_detail = None
if isinstance(loans, list) and len(loans) > 0:
    first_loan = loans[0]
    loan_id = first_loan.get('loan_id') if isinstance(first_loan, dict) else None
    if loan_id:
        loan_detail = users.get_user_loan('${existing_user_primary_id}', str(loan_id))
passCriteria: first_loan is None or (isinstance(loan_detail, dict) and 'loan_id' in loan_detail)

tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #40 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises: list_user_loans on empty/None/non-string user_id; get_user_loan on empty/None user_id and empty/None loan_id; create_user_loan on empty/None user_id, on BOTH item_barcode AND item_pid supplied (must raise — exclusivity guard), on NEITHER item_barcode NOR item_pid supplied (must raise — at-least-one guard), and on non-dict loan_data when item_barcode is supplied; renew_user_loan on empty/None user_id and empty/None loan_id; update_user_loan on empty/None user_id, empty/None loan_id, empty dict loan_data, and non-dict loan_data. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers issue #40 AC 'Unit tests cover: missing/both item_barcode+item_pid raises AlmaValidationError; missing body fields raise AlmaValidationError' for the live runtime — these are pure Python guards but exercising them through the live Users object proves the guards are wired in the build the operator will actually use.
pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import AlmaValidationError
users = Users(client)
errors = {}

tests.scope: Operator-authorized live round-trip: create a real loan on ${existing_user_primary_id} for the supplied loanable item (${existing_item_pid} / holdings ${existing_holding_id} / MMS ${existing_mms_id}) at the operator-supplied test library and circ-desk, GET it, RENEW it, then RETURN it via BibliographicRecords.scan_in_item (op=scan + circ_desk = circulation return). The loans API itself has no DELETE endpoint, but the same Alma endpoint family that places work-orders also performs circ-desk returns when called with a circ_desk and no work_order_type — so the cleanup IS automatable via an existing toolkit method. Sequence: (1) create_user_loan(${existing_user_primary_id}, item_pid=${existing_item_pid}, loan_data={'circ_desk': {'value': '${existing_circ_desk_code}'}, 'library': {'value': '${target_library_code}'}}) — capture loan_id from response.data; (2) get_user_loan asserts the response echoes loan_id and item_pid; (3) renew_user_loan exercises op=renew; (4) bibs.scan_in_item(${existing_mms_id}, ${existing_holding_id}, ${existing_item_pid}, library=${target_library_code}, circ_desk=${existing_circ_desk_code}) returns the item; (5) the loan is now completed — operator's user-history shows: loan / renew / return — net state clean. update_user_loan (PUT — change due date) is INTENTIONALLY NOT exercised in the round-trip; see unmappable[]. The test prints loan_id, user_id, item_pid, holding_id, mms_id at runtime; if the scan-in step in the cleanup block fails for any reason, the loan is still on the user and the test re-prints those identifiers as a manual-cleanup banner.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read-only and write smoke testing. Used by t-40-1 (read-only list/get) and t-40-3 (live create→get→renew→return loan round-trip with automatic cleanup via scan-in). Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

An existing Alma item PID in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for a live loan create→renew→return round-trip against ${existing_user_primary_id}. The item must be currently on-shelf and belong to ${target_library_code} so that the scan-in cleanup step (circ-desk return) completes the loan rather than placing the item in transit. Synthetic placeholder only; the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime. Never write a real item PID into this file.

The Alma holdings_id under which ${existing_item_pid} lives. Required by the scan_in_item cleanup step at the end of t-40-3, which targets POST /almaws/v1/bibs/{mms_id}/holdings/{holdings_id}/items/{item_pid}?op=scan. Synthetic placeholder only.
The Alma MMS ID of the bib record that owns ${existing_item_pid}. Required by the scan_in_item cleanup step. Synthetic placeholder only.

The library code that owns ${existing_item_pid}; the operator has circulation rights here. Used (1) in the loan-body for create_user_loan, and (2) as the library param on the scan_in_item cleanup step that returns the loaned item. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

A circulation-desk code at ${target_library_code} that the supplied SANDBOX operator has rights to. Used (1) in the loan-body for create_user_loan, and (2) as the circ_desk param on the scan_in_item cleanup step (which performs a circulation return when called with a circ_desk and no work_order_type). Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real circ-desk code into this file.

pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
from almaapitk.domains.bibs import BibliographicRecords
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError
users = Users(client)
bibs = BibliographicRecords(client)

create_response = None
create_data = None
loan_id = None
get_response = None
get_loan_id_echo = None
renew_response = None
scan_in_response = None
print(f'[t-40-3] live create→renew→return on user ${existing_user_primary_id}, item ${existing_item_pid} at library ${target_library_code} / circ_desk ${existing_circ_desk_code}')

create_data = getattr(create_response, 'data', None) if create_response is not None else None
if isinstance(create_data, dict):
    loan_id = create_data.get('loan_id')
print(f'[t-40-3] loan created: loan_id={loan_id!r} user_id=${existing_user_primary_id} item_pid=${existing_item_pid}')

renew_response = users.renew_user_loan('${existing_user_primary_id}', str(loan_id)) if loan_id else None
print(f'[t-40-3] loan renewed: loan_id={loan_id!r}')

# Cleanup-in-band: return the item via scan-in (circ-desk scan).
# Same Alma endpoint used for work-order placement also performs
# circulation returns when called with circ_desk and no work_order_type.
scan_in_response = bibs.scan_in_item(
    mms_id='${existing_mms_id}',
    holding_id='${existing_holding_id}',
    item_pid='${existing_item_pid}',
    library='${target_library_code}',
    circ_desk='${existing_circ_desk_code}',
)
print(f'[t-40-3] item returned via scan-in: status={getattr(scan_in_response, "status_code", None)} success={getattr(scan_in_response, "success", None)}')
passCriteria: create_response is not None and getattr(create_response, 'success', False) is True
isinstance(create_data, dict) and 'loan_id' in create_data and loan_id is not None
renew_response is not None and getattr(renew_response, 'success', False) is True
scan_in_response is not None and getattr(scan_in_response, 'success', False) is True
cleanup:
# Belt-and-braces: if the in-band scan-in did not run (e.g. an
# earlier assertion raised) but the loan was created, retry the
# scan-in once. If it still fails, print a manual-cleanup banner
# with the loan_id, user_id, item_pid so the operator can clear
# the leftover loan via the Alma staff UI.
try:
    if loan_id and (scan_in_response is None or not getattr(scan_in_response, 'success', False)):
        bibs2 = BibliographicRecords(client)
        bibs2.scan_in_item(
            mms_id='${existing_mms_id}',
            holding_id='${existing_holding_id}',
            item_pid='${existing_item_pid}',
            library='${target_library_code}',
            circ_desk='${existing_circ_desk_code}',
        )
        print(f'[t-40-3 cleanup] in-finally scan-in returned the item OK')
except AlmaAPIError as cleanup_err:
    print(f'\n!!! [t-40-3] MANUAL CLEANUP REQUIRED !!!\n!!! loan_id: {loan_id!r}\n!!! user_id: ${existing_user_primary_id}\n!!! item_pid: ${existing_item_pid}\n!!! reason: {cleanup_err}\n!!! Operator must return the item via the Alma staff UI.\n')

unmappable.ac: `list_user_loans` returns the Alma envelope and supports
unmappable.reason: Partially mapped: t-40-1 exercises the live GET /users/{user_id}/loans path with the default limit=10 / offset=0 and asserts the wrapper returns a List[Dict]. The 'supports limit / offset / expand / order_by query params' facet is NOT individually exercised against SANDBOX because the test user is expected to have zero or very few loans, making pagination/sort behaviour unobservable. The query-string assembly for these four params is pinned by the chunk's mocked-HTTP unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py; this entry documents that residual unit-only facet.

unmappable.ac: `change_loan_due_date` performs a PUT with a properly formatted ISO
unmappable.reason: DELIBERATE SCOPE LIMIT: update_user_loan (PUT /users/{user_id}/loans/{loan_id} — the wrapper that the issue spec refers to as 'change_loan_due_date'; it was renamed update_user_loan during implementation to match the Alma swagger 'Update Loan' shape) is INTENTIONALLY NOT exercised against SANDBOX. Two reasons: (1) Alma exposes NO DELETE endpoint for loans (verified live this session) — t-40-3 already creates a loan that persists until manual operator intervention, and adding a PUT mutation on the same loan would not change the cleanup picture but would add another mutation that the regression-smoke output has to flag. (2) The PUT wire shape (path-binding + JSON body verbatim + due_date ISO-8601-with-Z formatting) is fully pinned by the chunk's mocked-HTTP unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py — SANDBOX cannot distinguish between two body shapes that both happen to be accepted, so a live PUT would only reproduce the unit-test guarantee at the cost of a second mutation. The validation guards on update_user_loan (empty user_id / loan_id / loan_data, non-dict loan_data) ARE exercised by t-40-2 against the live Users object.

The 'unit tests (mocked HTTP)' facet — specifically the wire-shape pins for op=renew query string assembly, and the date-formatting tests for str-vs-datetime inputs to update_user_loan / change_loan_due_date — is a local pytest-suite deliverable (tests/unit/domains/test_users.py) verified pre-merge by the test runner, not by Alma SANDBOX behaviour. The validation half (missing/both item_barcode+item_pid raises AlmaValidationError; missing-body-fields raises AlmaValidationError) IS exercised by t-40-2 against the live Users object; the wire-correctness of op=renew IS exercised end-to-end by t-40-3 (renew_user_loan only succeeds if the wrapper actually appends ?op=renew); this entry documents the residual unit-only facet (date-formatting + mocked-HTTP wire pins for the variants not exercised live).

unmappable.ac: At least one SANDBOX integration test for the full create→renew→PUT
unmappable.reason: Partially mapped: t-40-3 exercises create→get→renew live against the supplied SANDBOX user + item PID. The PUT (update_user_loan / change_loan_due_date) leg of the cycle is NOT exercised live for the reasons documented under the change_loan_due_date AC above (Alma has no DELETE for loans, so adding a PUT mutation would only duplicate the mocked-HTTP unit-test guarantee at the cost of further state). The 'full create→renew→PUT cycle' AC is therefore mapped to t-40-3 for the create+renew portion and to the chunk's mocked-HTTP unit tests for the PUT portion.

Logging-call invariant on the source. SANDBOX returns identical bytes regardless of whether the wrapper logs or prints. Observable only by inspecting the source (no `print(` tokens in the new methods) and by reviewing the `logs/` directory after the chunk runs. Verified by code review of the chunk diff. (Note: the t-40-3 print() statements are inside the SANDBOX-test runner script — emitted to stdout for operator-cleanup visibility — not inside the wrapper methods on src/almaapitk/domains/users.py. The AC is about the wrapper, which uses self.logger throughout.)

Local-suite invariant. Both are run by the operator (and CI) in the local environment; no Alma SANDBOX call would observe their outcome. Verified pre-merge by `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and `poetry run pytest tests/test_public_api_contract.py -v`, not by SANDBOX behaviour.
